input.background: The table below shows data from an experiment to compare resting metabolic rates (kcal/min) in five volunteers each given two diets, a normal diet and an overfeeding diet which contained 50% more energy (Welle et al., 1986). Data were collected before and after eating a meal.
input.data:
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Subject & Diet & Before meal & After meal \\
\hline 1 & N & 1.47 & 1.78 \\
\hline & O & 1.72 & 2.49 \\
\hline 2 & N & 1.42 & 1.68 \\
\hline & O & 1.44 & 1.87 \\
\hline 3 & N & 1.10 & 1.26 \\
\hline & O & 1.11 & 1.36 \\
\hline 4 & N & 0.84 & 1.11 \\
\hline & O & 0.90 & 1.29 \\
\hline 5 & N & 0.91 & 1.09 \\
\hline & O & 1.00 & 1.25 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

N : Normal diet: O : Overfed diet
input.question: What methods of analysis could be used to examine the difference between the metabolic rates in relation to diet for the post-prandial data?

output.answer: A paired t test or, equivalently, a two way analysis of variance.
